project.name: BMP :: bmp-monitoring (health + logs dashboard)
project.description:
Session 7. Spring Boot Admin server — auto-discovers every Eureka-registered BMP service (no separate client dependency needed on each one; discovery reads the registry directly) and shows health, metrics, env, loggers (view/change log levels live), and thread/heap dumps for each in one dashboard. This IS the "monitoring service" — deliberately NOT a Prometheus/Grafana/Loki stack, which would mean standing up and paying for infrastructure this pre-PMF team doesn't need yet; Spring Boot Admin needs nothing beyond this one Spring Boot app plus the actuator endpoints every service already exposes.
de.codecentric:spring-boot-admin-starter-server version pinned directly on the dependency below (not resolved via the root pom's dependencyManagement, since it isn't part of the spring-cloud-dependencies BOM already imported there) — chosen to match Spring Boot 3.4.1's line, NOT verified against a live `mvn dependency:tree` (no `mvn` binary available in the sandbox this was built in). Confirm the version resolves cleanly on first real build; bump if codecentric has since released a patch specifically validated against 3.4.1.
